name: Dark Mode Switcher
task: Implement a dark mode toggle for the W Construction website with persistent preference, system preference detection, and smooth transitions across all pages.

constraints: Use Tailwind dark mode with class strategy
All pages must be fully themed
definitionOfDone: All pages render correctly in both modes
No flash of wrong theme on load

passCriteria: All pages visually verified in both modes
knownLimitations: Third-party embeds may not respect dark mode

dev.output: 13 files changed. Dark mode toggle added to all pages. Tailwind darkMode: 'class' configured in tailwind.config.ts.

qa.output: 100 Playwright tests across 5 device viewports. FAIL - 4 P1 defects, 3 P2 defects. Root cause: duplicate Tailwind config files (tailwind.config.js overriding tailwind.config.ts).

defects.description: Services dropdown: white background in dark mode
Contact form inputs: white background, invisible text (~1.3:1 contrast)
Logo swap: dark:hidden/dark:block not applying (root cause: dual config)
Service card headings: near-invisible text on dark backgrounds
Contact form labels: low contrast in dark mode
Contact info card: light background in dark mode
Mobile theme switcher: cut off on iPhone SE below social icons

findings.description: Services dropdown: white background in dark mode
Contact form inputs: white background, invisible text (~1.3:1 contrast)
Logo swap: dark:hidden/dark:block not applying (root cause: dual config)
Service card headings: near-invisible text on dark backgrounds
Contact form labels: low contrast in dark mode
Contact info card: light background in dark mode
Mobile theme switcher: cut off on iPhone SE below social icons

graderResults.summary: 100 Playwright tests across 5 device viewports. Root cause identified: duplicate Tailwind config files. The tailwind.config.js is overriding tailwind.config.ts, causing all dark: class variants to silently fail.

findings.description: Two Tailwind config files coexist (tailwind.config.js and tailwind.config.ts). The .js file takes precedence at runtime, causing the darkMode: 'class' setting in .ts to be silently ignored. This is the root cause of all dark mode failures.
findings.suggestion: Remove tailwind.config.js entirely. Only tailwind.config.ts should exist. Verify with: ls tailwind.config.*

findings.description: ThemeSwitcher lacks an error boundary. If next-themes fails to initialize (e.g., SSR hydration mismatch), the toggle disappears with no fallback UI.
findings.suggestion: Wrap ThemeSwitcher in a React ErrorBoundary or add a try/catch around the useTheme() hook with a fallback render.

findings.description: Dark mode color values are hardcoded inline across multiple components (e.g., bg-gray-900, text-gray-100). Consider extracting these to CSS custom properties or a Tailwind theme extension for consistency and easier future updates.
findings.suggestion: Define a color palette in tailwind.config.ts under theme.extend.colors and reference named tokens instead of raw gray values.

graderResults.summary: Code review identified the dual Tailwind config as the root cause of all dark mode failures — a systemic issue rather than per-component bugs. One structural issue (duplicate config), one resilience gap (missing error boundary), one style observation.

dev.output: Root cause fix: removed duplicate tailwind.config.js. Fixed all P1 and P2 defects.

qa.output: Full re-verification. All previously failing tests now pass. No new defects found. PASS.

graderResults.summary: Full re-verification complete. All 100 Playwright tests pass. Dark mode works correctly across all pages and viewports. No new defects found.

findings.description: CR-02 from round 1 (missing error boundary on ThemeSwitcher) remains unresolved but is acceptable for this sprint scope. Recommend addressing in a follow-up.
findings.suggestion: Add ErrorBoundary wrapper in a future sprint.

graderResults.summary: Duplicate config file removed. Dark mode logic is now clean and correct. The minor error boundary gap was not addressed but is not blocking. Code quality is solid overall.
